subject: [PATCH v2] mm: migrate: requeue destination folio on deferred split queue

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David pointed out that the folio_test_large_rmappable() check is unnecessary, as other migrated folios would have their _deferred_list initialized but unused.
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think the folio_test_large_rmappable() check is required. Other
folios we migrate here would always have _deferred_list initialized but
unused.

Acked-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the THP shrinker code still requires a fix, pointing to another patch they've sent for review.
reviews.raw_body:
I have been auditing the THP shrinker code when it comes to NUMA migration and I think we need
another fix for this. I have sent it here https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260311132342.3193160-1-usama.arif@linux.dev/

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's migration code where destination folios were not being requeued on the deferred split queue after a successful migration. The source folio is checked to see if it was on the deferred split queue before moving it, and if so, the destination folio is requeued with its partially mapped state. This ensures that underutilized THPs do not escape the shrinker after NUMA migration.
